MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANT LORETTA BIRMINGHAM'S Motion For Relief From Default & Default Judgment IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-11-2009 Off Calendar

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Continued To Jul-23-09 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 612

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Continued To Jul-23-09 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 612

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Mar-12-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Douglas Holloway, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Claas Financial Services, LLC.
No appearance by defendants JW Grand Canyon Dairy, LLC or Loretta Birmingham.
Defaults were entered against defendants on February 10, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on February 10, 2009.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on March 10, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Claas Financial Services, LLC, and against the Defaulted Defendants JW Grand Canyon Dairy, LLC and Loretta Birmingham as follows:

Damages/Principal - $260,497.73.
Prejudgment Interest - $5,139.27.
Attorney's Fees - $2,500.00.
Costs - $505.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $268,642.00.

It is further Ordered and Adjudged that plaintiff Claas Financial Services, LLC is the owner of the leased equipment listed in Paragraph 3 of the Judgment.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
